metadata.title: Testing of Retroviral Vector-Based Human Gene Therapy Products for Replication Competent Retrovirus (RCR) During Product Manufacture and Patient Follow-up

rules.rule_text: The Virus Producing Cell (VPC) Master Cell Bank (MCB) must be tested for RCR by testing both cells and supernatant, using a cell line based on the tropism of the parental retrovirus.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend testing the VPC MCB for RCR by testing both cells and supernatant, using a cell line based on the tropism of the parental retrovirus.

rules.rule_text: RCR testing of the Working Cell Bank (WCB) is NOT recommended unless MCB testing is insufficient to ensure absence of RCR.
rules.verbatim_quote: Unlike the 2006 guidance, we are no longer recommending RCR testing of the VPC WCB.
rules.exceptions: Testing may be warranted if MCB testing is insufficient to provide assurance of absence of RCR.

rules.rule_text: Both the retroviral vector supernatant lots AND the End-of-Production (EOP) cells must be tested for RCR at product release.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend testing both the retroviral vector supernatant lots and EOP cells for RCR at product release.

rules.rule_text: Each lot of ex vivo retroviral transduced cells must be tested for RCR, regardless of the length of time the cells are cultured after transduction.
rules.source_section: III.A.4 Ex Vivo Retroviral Transduced Cells
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that each lot of ex vivo retroviral transduced cells be tested for RCR. This recommendation applies regardless of the length of time that the cells are cultured after transduction.

rules.rule_text: The volume of retroviral vector supernatant tested must be sufficient to achieve at least a 95% probability of detecting 1 RCR per dose equivalent, calculated using the formula: Vt = -(1/c)ln(1-p), where Vt is volume tested, c is the concentration of RCR, and p is the probability of detection.
rules.verbatim_quote: The volume of retroviral vector supernatant tested should be sufficient to provide at least a 95% probability of detecting 1 RCR per dose equivalent... Vt = -(1/c)ln(1-p)

rules.rule_text: For cell-based RCR testing, the number of cells tested must be 1% of the total cells in the lot or 10^8 cells, whichever is less.
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of cells tested should be 1% of the total cells in the lot or 10^8 cells, whichever is less.

rules.rule_text: RCR assays must be performed by culturing test material on a permissive cell line for a minimum of 5 passages, and all assays must include both relevant positive and negative controls.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assays for RCR testing should involve culturing the test material on a permissive cell line for a minimum of 5 passages. All assays should include relevant positive and negative controls.
rules.exceptions: Alternative methods (e.g., PCR) may be acceptable for ex vivo cell lot release under certain conditions.

rules.rule_text: All RCR assays must include an inhibitory control consisting of a relevant positive control spiked into the vector supernatant to detect assay inhibition.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assays should include an inhibitory control (a relevant positive control spiked into the vector supernatant) to detect assay inhibition.

rules.rule_text: Alternative methods for RCR detection (e.g., PCR-based assays) are acceptable for ex vivo transduced cell lot release when time constraints prevent culture-based testing, provided that data demonstrating sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility are submitted and the method is developed in consultation with CBER.
rules.verbatim_quote: Alternative methods (e.g., PCR) may be acceptable for ex vivo cells lot release when time constraints do not permit the use of culture-based assays. Sponsors should provide data on sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility of the alternative assay and should develop the method in consultation with CBER.
rules.exceptions: Applicable only when time constraints exist for ex vivo cell products; not a general substitute for culture-based assays.

rules.rule_text: Patients treated with retroviral vector-based gene therapy products must be monitored for RCR on the following schedule: pre-treatment baseline, then at 3, 6, and 12 months post-treatment, then yearly for up to 15 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should be monitored for RCR at the following time points: pre-treatment, then at 3, 6, and 12 months post-treatment, then yearly for up to 15 years.
rules.exceptions: If all year-1 post-treatment assays are negative, yearly sample collection may be discontinued; yearly medical history review is sufficient thereafter.

rules.rule_text: If all patient RCR assay results at pre-treatment and 3, 6, and 12 months post-treatment are negative, yearly sample collection may be discontinued; however, yearly medical history review must continue for the full 15-year monitoring period.
rules.verbatim_quote: If all year-1 post-treatment assays are negative, we recommend that yearly sample collection be discontinued and that medical history review be conducted yearly for up to 15 years.
rules.exceptions: Does not apply if any year-1 assay is positive; full schedule must continue.

rules.rule_text: Recommended patient assays for RCR monitoring include both serologic detection of RCR-specific antibodies AND analysis of peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) by PCR for RCR-specific DNA.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend both serologic detection of RCR-specific antibodies and analysis of PBMCs by PCR for RCR-specific DNA.

rules.rule_text: Positive RCR results from production testing must be documented immediately in an IND amendment submitted as an IND safety report per 21 CFR 312.32.
rules.source_section: V. Documentation of RCR Testing Results
rules.verbatim_quote: Positive results from production RCR testing should be documented in an IND amendment submitted as an IND safety report (21 CFR 312.32).
rules.exceptions: Negative results are reported in the annual IND report (21 CFR 312.33), not as safety reports.

rules.rule_text: Positive RCR results from patient monitoring must be documented immediately in an IND amendment submitted as an IND safety report per 21 CFR 312.32.
rules.source_section: V. Documentation of RCR Testing Results
rules.verbatim_quote: Positive results from patient RCR testing should be documented in an IND amendment submitted as an IND safety report (21 CFR 312.32).
rules.exceptions: Negative patient results are reported in the annual IND report (21 CFR 312.33).

rules.rule_text: Negative RCR results from both production testing and patient monitoring must be reported in the annual IND progress report per 21 CFR 312.33.
rules.source_section: V. Documentation of RCR Testing Results
rules.verbatim_quote: Negative results from production and patient RCR testing should be reported in the annual IND progress report (21 CFR 312.33).
rules.exceptions: Positive results require immediate IND safety report (21 CFR 312.32), not annual reporting.

rules.rule_text: If RCR testing is discontinued for a patient (per the year-1 negative results protocol), collected samples must be archived for at least 6 months after the product expiration date.
rules.verbatim_quote: Samples collected but not tested should be archived for at least 6 months after the product expiration date.

rules.rule_text: Post-licensure, RCR testing during manufacture must continue; the Biologics License Application (BLA) must include a provision to test for RCR upon development of any retrovirus-associated disease in treated patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: Post-licensure, RCR testing during manufacture should continue. The BLA should include a provision to test for RCR upon development of retrovirus-associated disease.

rules.rule_text: Post-licensure, patients who die from a retrovirus-associated disease or who develop neoplasms within 15 years of treatment must have biopsy and/or autopsy tissue assayed for RCR.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients who die from a retrovirus-associated disease or who develop neoplasms within 15 years of treatment should have biopsy and/or autopsy tissue assayed for RCR.

rules.rule_text: Licensed retroviral vector gene therapy product labeling must incorporate RCR risk information.
rules.verbatim_quote: Product labeling should incorporate RCR risk information.

rules.rule_text: The VPC MCB RCR test must use a permissive cell line selected based on the tropism of the parental retrovirus; a cell line that does not support the tropism of the parental virus is not acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: using a cell line based on the tropism of the parental retrovirus
